name: SWM System Architecture
description: A comprehensive TypeScript system for extracting, structuring, analyzing, and serving world models from diverse inputs using LLM agents and graph analysis

entities.name: CLI Interface
entities.description: Command-line interface providing 20+ commands for building, inspecting, merging, and transforming world models

entities.name: Pipeline System
entities.description: Orchestrates the multi-stage world model extraction process with callbacks and progress tracking

entities.name: Extraction Agent
entities.description: LLM-powered agent that analyzes raw input and extracts entities, relations, processes, and constraints

entities.name: Structuring Agent
entities.description: Agent that converts raw LLM extractions into properly structured world models with normalized types and IDs

entities.name: Validation Agent
entities.description: Agent that performs integrity checks on world models, detecting orphans, dangling references, and circular dependencies

entities.name: Second Pass Agent
entities.description: Agent that finds implicit entities and relations missed in the first extraction pass

entities.name: Query Agent
entities.description: Agent that answers natural language questions about world models using graph patterns and LLM inference

entities.name: Refinement Agent
entities.description: Agent that incrementally updates existing world models with new input

entities.name: Transform Agent
entities.description: Agent that applies natural language transformations to modify world models

entities.name: Chunker Module
entities.description: Splits large inputs into token-sized chunks with paragraph-boundary awareness and overlap

entities.name: LLM Client
entities.description: Anthropic Claude API client with retry logic, timeout handling, and token estimation

entities.name: Graph Analysis Module
entities.description: Provides pathfinding, dependency analysis, impact analysis, clustering, and subgraph extraction

entities.name: World Model Schema
entities.description: Zod-based schema defining entities, relations, processes, constraints with validation

entities.name: MCP Server
entities.description: Model Context Protocol server exposing world model as 9 live queryable tools for AI agents

entities.name: Export System
entities.description: Converts world models to Claude MD, system prompts, MCP schemas, Mermaid diagrams, and DOT graphs

entities.name: Timeline System
entities.description: Tracks world model evolution over time with snapshots and automatic diffing

entities.name: Merge System
entities.description: Combines multiple world models with entity deduplication and ID remapping

entities.name: Fix System
entities.description: Auto-repairs world model validation issues like orphan entities and dangling references

entities.name: Entity
entities.description: Core domain object representing actors, systems, concepts, locations, events, groups, objects, or resources

entities.name: Relation
entities.description: Directed connection between entities with semantic types like depends_on, uses, contains

entities.name: Process
entities.description: Dynamic sequence of ordered steps with actors, triggers, and outcomes

entities.name: Constraint
entities.description: Invariant rule that must hold true, with hard or soft severity

entities.name: Prompt Templates
entities.description: Specialized extraction prompts for different source types (text, code, conversation, document, URL)

entities.name: File Input
entities.description: Text files, code files, JSON, YAML, CSV, and other document formats

entities.name: URL Input
entities.description: Web pages fetched and stripped of HTML for content extraction

entities.name: Validation Result
entities.description: Comprehensive validation report with issues, stats, and quality scores

entities.name: Query Result
entities.description: Answer to natural language questions with method, confidence, and referenced entities

processes.name: World Model Extraction
processes.description: Complete pipeline to extract structured world models from any input type
processes.trigger: User runs swm model command with input

steps.action: Detect source type from file extension or content patterns
Check input size and chunk if necessary
Extract entities, relations, processes, constraints using specialized prompts
Structure raw extraction into proper world model with ID generation
Validate model integrity and generate quality score

outcomes: Structured world model with validation report

processes.name: Multi-Pass Extraction
processes.description: Enhanced extraction with multiple passes to find implicit elements

steps.action: Find implicit entities and relations missed in first pass

outcomes: More complete world model with implicit elements captured

processes.name: Natural Language Query
processes.description: Answer questions about world models using graph patterns and LLM inference
processes.trigger: User asks question via swm query command

steps.action: Try to match question against known graph patterns
If pattern matched, execute graph analysis
If no pattern match, use LLM inference with model context
Format answer with method, confidence, and referenced entities

outcomes: Natural language answer with confidence and method

processes.name: MCP Tool Execution
processes.description: Serve world model as live tools for AI agents via Model Context Protocol

steps.action: Execute appropriate graph analysis or query
Format result for AI agent consumption

outcomes: Real-time domain expertise for AI agents

constraints.name: Token Limit Constraint
constraints.description: Input chunks must not exceed 80,000 tokens to fit within Claude's context window

constraints.name: Entity Name Uniqueness
constraints.description: Entity names within a world model should be unique after normalization

constraints.name: Relation Reference Integrity
constraints.description: All relation source and target IDs must reference existing entities

constraints.name: Process Step Ordering
constraints.description: Process steps must have monotonically increasing order numbers

constraints.name: Schema Compliance
constraints.description: All world models must validate against the Zod schema

constraints.name: API Rate Limiting
constraints.description: Claude API calls must respect rate limits with exponential backoff retry

constraints.name: Source Type Detection
constraints.description: Input must be classified into one of six source types for appropriate prompt selection

constraints.name: Confidence Scoring
constraints.description: All extractions must include confidence scores between 0 and 1

metadata.source_summary: Analysis of complete SWM codebase including CLI, agents, pipeline, schema, utilities, and server components
extraction_notes: System implements sophisticated multi-pass extraction with chunk handling
Strong emphasis on validation and auto-fixing of model integrity issues
Provides both CLI and MCP server interfaces for different use cases
Graph analysis capabilities include pathfinding, clustering, and impact analysis
Export system supports multiple formats for AI consumption (Claude MD, system prompts, MCP schemas)
